summary: Implement M3 appointments, enfeoffment, policies, and reason scaffolds
acceptance_criteria: Versioned GameCommand paths support appointment, bulk appointment, enfeoffment, and office/jurisdiction policy updates, and the same command family is usable by player and AI actors.

Command validation checks office eligibility, mutually exclusive primary offices, jurisdiction existence, character liveness/location constraints, and authority from polity/office rather than UI state.
Policies remain attached to offices or jurisdictions when the holder changes; holder ability and relationships change execution performance and visible reason codes.
Minimal character proposal/reason scaffolds exist for appointment, policy, and enfeoffment decisions without exposing hidden full weights or creating autonomous out-of-scope gameplay.
Bulk commands are deterministic, auditable, and fail with stable per-item rejection reasons instead of partially mutating in an implicit order.
No client UI, full AI planner, full diplomacy, full war loop, formal historical content, or new production dependency is introduced.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover office exclusivity, dead/invalid character rejection, policy persistence across holder changes, deterministic bulk command ordering, and stable reason codes.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-APPOINTMENT-ENFEOFFMENT-001
notes: This is the sim/protocol foundation for the later client appointment/bulk/reason UI task.
Do not put domain rules in client read models; client work must consume protocol/query outputs from this task.
If proposal behavior grows beyond minimal M3 reason scaffolds, stop and route to gameplay_designer/systems_architect before widening scope.
